title: SSO redirect loop for users with plus-addressed or aliased emails after Okta-to-Entra migration
problem: After migrating SSO from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID, approximately 30% of users are stuck in an infinite redirect loop: they authenticate successfully at Microsoft, get redirected back to TaskFlow, but TaskFlow immediately redirects them back to Microsoft. The loop repeats indefinitely. Affected users can log into other Entra-backed apps without issue.
root_cause_hypothesis: TaskFlow's SSO callback handler fails to match the authenticated user's identity from the Entra ID token to a local TaskFlow account. Two likely sub-causes: (1) Plus-addressed emails (e.g., jane+taskflow@company.com) — the '+' character may be URL-decoded as a space during the callback, or Entra may normalize/strip the plus-address portion, causing a mismatch against the stored email. (2) Email aliases — users whose primary email changed during the Entra migration have a different email in the OIDC/SAML token than what TaskFlow has stored from the Okta era. In both cases, the lookup fails, no session is created, and TaskFlow treats the user as unauthenticated, restarting the login flow.
reproduction_steps: Configure TaskFlow SSO with Microsoft Entra ID
Create or use a test user whose email contains '+' (e.g., testuser+tag@company.com) or whose Entra primary email differs from the email stored in TaskFlow
Attempt to log in to TaskFlow via SSO
Observe: authentication at Microsoft succeeds, redirect back to TaskFlow occurs, but TaskFlow immediately redirects back to Microsoft, creating an infinite loop
environment: TaskFlow with SSO, recently migrated from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID. Affects multiple browsers (Chrome, Edge, Firefox). Server-side issue, not client-specific.
impact: ~30% of the team is completely locked out of TaskFlow. No workaround available for affected users short of changing their email addresses.
recommended_fix: Investigate the SSO callback handler's user-matching logic. Specifically: (1) Check how the email claim from the Entra ID token is compared against stored user emails — ensure '+' characters are preserved and properly handled (not URL-decoded to spaces). (2) Add support for matching on multiple email attributes or aliases, or add a migration step that updates stored emails to match Entra primary emails. (3) Consider matching on a stable, provider-agnostic identifier (like a user GUID or immutable username) rather than email alone, to prevent this class of issue during future identity provider changes. Add logging in the SSO callback to record the received email claim and the lookup result so matching failures are visible.
proposed_test_case: Write integration tests for the SSO callback user-matching logic that cover: (a) a user with a plus-addressed email authenticates and is correctly matched, (b) a user whose stored email differs from their Entra primary email is correctly matched via alias or secondary lookup, (c) when no match is found, the user receives a clear error message instead of a redirect loop.
information_gaps: Which SSO protocol TaskFlow uses (OIDC vs SAML) and which specific claim/assertion carries the email
Whether TaskFlow's user lookup is email-based or uses another identifier
Exact server-side logs from a failed login attempt showing the received token claims and lookup query
Whether the few affected users without '+' in their email all fall into the 'email changed during migration' category
